objective: Modernize the color palette in redesign-v4v1.html to use a clean, professional tone (Slate and Indigo) instead of chaotic 'rainbow' brand colors.

completed: Wrote a Python script to comprehensively map disparate, highly-saturated background colors (red, pink, orange, cyan) to unified Slate (50/100) and Indigo (50) backgrounds.
Mapped border and highlight colors to Slate 800 (for primary typography), Indigo 600 (for badges/prices), and Slate 200 (for subtle borders).
Updated the primary CTA KakaoTalk button to an elegant Indigo 600 with an adjusted box-shadow for prominence.
Verified formatting syntax to maintain valid HTML/CSS structure without breaking existing tags.

decisions_locked: Used Indigo 600 (#4f46e5) as the primary unified accent color and Slate 800 (#1e293b) as the core structural/typographical color.
Swapped the global background (#e5e7eb) to a slightly lighter Slate 100 (#f1f5f9) for a cleaner SaaS aesthetic.
acceptance_criteria: HTML file correctly renders without any hardcoded highly-saturated brand colors (except for the brand logo).
Linter and unit tests pass successfully without issues.
practical_recommendations: The new UI feels significantly more premium and trustworthy. Future ad templates should adhere to this Slate + Indigo color token system instead of copying original vendor brand colors directly.
next_actions: Review the updated HTML in a browser to confirm the aesthetic aligns perfectly with expectations. If satisfied, deploy.
